subject: Astronomy & Space
title: Georges Lemaître: The Father of the Big Bang
description: Learn Georges Lemaître: The Father of the Big Bang in one minute.

hook.text: A Belgian priest first proposed that the universe began from a 'primeval atom.' Physicists laughed at him.

buildup.text: Georges Lemaître published his idea in 1927—two years before Hubble's observations supported it.

discovery.text: He derived the expanding universe from Einstein's equations. Einstein dismissed it: 'Your physics is abominable.'

twist.text: Fred Hoyle coined 'Big Bang' as a mocking term on BBC radio in 1949. The name stuck despite the ridicule.

climax.text: Lemaître lived to see the CMB discovery in 1965, finally proving his theory correct. He died in 1966.

punchline.text: A priest proposed the Big Bang. A critic accidentally named it.

quiz.question: Who first proposed that the universe expanded from a 'primeval atom'?
options: Georges Lemaître, a Belgian priest and physicist
Edwin Hubble, based on galaxy observations
Albert Einstein, from his theory of general relativity
